channel.title: Pharma AI & Data Watch
channel.description: Curated AI, pharma, and regulatory science intelligence — updated weekly.

item.title: CHIP-AML22: a complex clinical trial in de novo pediatric AML patients, including a gemtuzumab ozogamicin randomization and targeted therapy with quizartinib in eligible subgroups, within the NOPHO-DB-SHIP consortium
item.description: CHIP-AML22 is a complex clinical trial evaluating treatment strategies in newly diagnosed pediatric acute myeloid leukemia (AML) patients through a multi-center consortium. The trial includes a randomized assessment of gemtuzumab ozogamicin and targeted therapy with quizartinib for eligible patient

item.title: US curbs anthropic AI access, raising global concerns
item.description: The Trump administration has restricted foreign access to Anthropic's latest AI models over security fears, forcing a global shutdown. Could the row hurt the Claude-maker's IPO plans and set a far-reaching precedent? Silicon Valley executives and global polic…

item.title: Tech industry defector in ny-12 gets boost from new super pac
item.description: Guardrails Alliance has already raised $5 million and yesterday launched its first ad buys for New York state Rep. Alex Bores, who is running to replace longtime Rep. Jerry Nadler. The post Tech Industry Defector in NY-12 Gets Boost From New Super PAC appeare…

item.title: Keysight’s chief technologist on the path to 6g
item.description: Balaji Raghothaman, Keysight’s chief technologist for 6G, discusses what it takes for the telecoms industry to move to 6G, from supporting AI workloads to integrating with satellites connectivity and driving enterprise use cases

item.title: The week that changed AI: inside trump’s anthropic crackdown, and how a phone call from amazon CEO andy jassy triggered the chaos
item.description: The fight over Anthropic’s Mythos model is rewriting the rules of AI regulation, with consequences for the trillion dollar startup, the AI industry, and global security.

item.title: How a new york primary wound up at the center of the AI storm
item.description: Politics / June 19, 2026 How a New York Primary Wound Up at the Center of the AI Storm Alex Bores is engulfed in a brutal (and super-expensive) battle over how technology will change work, life, and society. John Nichols S…

item.title: A brain-computer interface has, for the first time, restored fluent everyday speech to a person who had lost it — a man with als who had not spoken aloud in years until a small implant from the braingate clinical trial began translating his attempts to speak directly into computer-synthesised words, in a result researchers reported in june 2026
item.description: Casey Harrell can now talk for twelve hours at a stretch. Two years ago, he could not talk at all. The ALS he was diagnosed with several years earlier had progressively destroyed the motor neurons controlling the muscles of his face and throat, leaving him wi…

item.title: Healthcare AI has left the lab. now comes the hard part.
item.description: Artificial intelligence in healthcare has evolved past a simple test-and-learn exercise. AI tools are now embedded into clinical scheduling, drug dispensing, patient communications and diagnostic decision-making across the industry, according to an analysis a…

item.title: The week in maternal-pediatric health tech (june 18) — the drought reaches week five: FDA pediatric label wave
item.description: Venture capital has now sat out five straight weeks in maternal-pediatric health. The FDA has not. On June 12 the agency cleared the first disease-modifying drug for newly diagnosed pediatric type 1 diabetes and the first over-the-counter glucose monitor clea…

item.title: Explore midjourney medical for ai-powered health scanning
item.description: Explore Midjourney Medical for AI-powered health scanningthedailyvibecode.vercel.app

item.title: Adeno-Associated Virus Gene Therapy Translation: Lessons from Early Regulatory Meetings
item.description: This article examines key insights from early regulatory meetings on adeno-associated virus (AAV) gene therapy development, drawing on experiences from the Therapeutic Development Branch and collaborating institutions to inform best practices for translation of AAV-based therapeutic approaches.

item.title: A startup claims it broke through a bottleneck that’s holding back LLMs
item.description: Miami-based AI startup Subquadratic came out of stealth mode last month with a huge claim. It announced that it had solved a mathematical bottleneck that had been holding back large language models for almost a decade. The details were thin, and many people were unconvinced. But Subquadratic has sta

item.title: Is Kaposi's sarcoma the end of the OX40/OX40L axis in atopic dermatitis?
item.description: The OX40/OX40L axis has shown durable activity in clinical development for atopic dermatitis with strong biological rationale, but questions have emerged regarding whether monotherapy benefits are sufficient compared to established and emerging treatment options. The development of this therapeutic

item.title: Systematic optimization of PD-L1 and CLDN18.2 CAR-T designs identifies a bicistronic dual-target, double-CD3ζ architecture with enhanced antitumor activity in gastric cancer
item.description: Researchers systematically optimized chimeric antigen receptor T-cell (CAR-T) designs targeting PD-L1 and CLDN18.2 to identify an enhanced architecture using a bicistronic construct with dual signaling domains (double-CD3ζ) for treating gastric cancer. This optimized dual-target, double-CD3ζ CAR-T d

item.title: Antony pegg: introducing coldfront: seamlessly uniting oltp, analytics and AI workloads on postgresql
item.description: Our team is excited to announce pgEdge ColdFront v1.0.0-beta1: open-source, transparent data tiering for PostgreSQL that unites OLTP, analytics and AI workloads, with no application code changes required. The headline feature: a fully writable cold tier. Jimm…

item.title: Why transparent AI matters for enterprise trust
item.description: Black box AI risks slowing adoption as enterprises demand transparency, governance and accountability.

item.title: What is artificial intelligence (AI)?
item.description: * AI’s impact comes from its breadth: the same pattern-recognition capabilities now power everything from fraud detection and medical imaging to personalization and content generation. * AI capability is advancing faster than AI reliability, making evaluatio…

item.title: France advances europe’s AI future with nvidia technologies
item.description: France laid out plans to advance local AI — from new AI factories and national compute capacity to open frontier models and industrial platforms. Now, that AI infrastructure is coming online. AI agents are running in production, startups are deploying applica…

item.title: Some states are forging ahead with AI regulations
item.description: Six months after President Donald Trump warned states not to regulate artificial intelligence, they are increasingly doing just that. Congress has stalled on producing federal regulation of artificial intelligence as states forge ahead and scrutinize how chat…

item.title: How inova modernized its data architecture to prepare for AI apps
item.description: Inova went through a data modernization effort to prepare the health system's data foundation to support the rollout of capabilities such as AI agents and ambient scribes.

item.title: Just 30% of financial firms use AI for customer retention
item.description: Enterprise artificial intelligence has moved past the pilot stage, but there are some challenges when it comes to gaining wider acceptance. The PYMNTS Intelligence report “Financial Services Pulls Ahead in the Enterprise AI Race” was based on a survey of 60 s…

item.title: Uae’s push towards agentic AI raises stakes for governance and accountability
item.description: As the UAE accelerates plans to embed autonomous AI into government services, experts warn that governance frameworks must evolve from policy documents into operational controls that ensure transparency, accountability and trust

item.title: Dpp readiness: when and how brands and suppliers can get started
item.description: This article outlines when and how brands and suppliers can prepare for the Digital Product Passport (DPP), a new EU regulation for product data.

item.title: Beyond oncogenesis: the unexplored benefits of viruses in cancer immunity
item.description: The study of tumour virology has traditionally focused on oncogenic viruses and their associated mechanisms of cancer initiation and promotion. Yet certain viral exposures can be beneficial by enhancing antitumour immunity. In this Review, Wang et al. explore…

item.title: Roachfest london 2026: the database as competitive asset
item.description: SPONSORED POST: Operate without fear. Build with confidence. Adapt to the AI era

item.title: Nasa picks eric schmidt's rocket company for mars mission, setting up a race with spacex | techcrunch
item.description: Relativity Space—a rocket maker acquired by former Google executive chair Eric Schmidt last year after stumbling on the path to orbit—might just beat SpaceX to Mars.

item.title: OpenAI is bringing on some big guns in the lead-up to its IPO
item.description: OpenAI is bulking up before its IPO, landing Transformer co-inventor Noam Shazeer from Google DeepMind and former Trump AI policy official Dean Ball in the same week.

item.title: The white house is making up its rules for AI in real time
item.description: Anthropic still can’t distribute Claude Mythos or Fable 5 after running afoul of the Trump administration. But no one can say exactly what the company did wrong.

item.title: Amazon employees say they’re facing termination for backing data center limits
item.description: When three Amazon software engineers testified earlier this month at Seattle City Council hearings about data centers, they started their testimony by citing a city law barring employment discrimination over political speech. Now, they're accusing their emplo…

item.title: Who decides when AI is too dangerous?
item.description: On today’s episode of Decoder, my guest is Hayden Field, senior AI reporter for The Verge. Often when Hayden comes on the show, it’s because something has gone wrong in the world of AI. Last weekend, that something was a pretty intense mix of Anthropic, the T…

item.title: Midjourney enters medical imaging with 60-second full-body scan
item.description: Midjourney built its reputation on text-to-image artificial intelligence. On Wednesday (June 17), the company announced in a blog post its first hardware product, the Midjourney Scanner. It’s a full-body ultrasound device that produces a complete body image i…

item.title: How to keep data silos from damaging your AI projects
item.description: Data silos impede successful AI operation. However, there are practical ways to connect data stores and improve AI outcomes.

item.title: Litecoin etf’s slow start shows altcoin funds still face a demand test
item.description: Canary Capital’s LTCC fund shows how far secondary crypto ETF demand still trails Bitcoin and Ethereum products, despite spot ETF availability.

item.title: New national digital health index identifies communities most at risk of being left behind in the digital health era
item.description: As telehealth, remote monitoring, and artificial intelligence-powered health tools become increasingly integrated into healthcare delivery, a new study published in JAMA Network Open introduces the first comprehensive national measure designed to assess wheth…

item.title: Before you buy the marketing agent, build the martech foundation
item.description: Gartner painted a compelling AI agent future. The gap between that vision and most marketing teams' operating reality was the real story. Continue reading...

item.title: Incor inaugurates cesin: unprecedented complex that integrates technology, science and high performance training
item.description: New complex of the Heart Institute (InCor-HCFMUSP) strengthens the training of professionals and the qualification of public health care in Brazil, by integrating immersive clinical simulation based on real situations and advanced training methodologies, for …

item.title: Lessons learned from our roundtable with rare disease advocates
item.description: Recently FDA leadership held a roundtable discussion with 15 dedicated rare disease leaders. Hearing directly from patients and family members who navigate these incredibly challenging conditions every day was both grounding and motivating.

item.title: The validity and reliability of a biometrically accurate, photorealistic set of young adult body size scales based on 3d scans of white europeans
item.description: Young adults are a high-risk group for weight-related health problems but are often overlooked in weight management intervention design. Validated visual tools that accurately depict body size are needed to improve communication and intervention strategies. T…

item.title: Stage-specific associations of mineralization markers with ckm syndrome: nationwide survey and genetic evidence for alkaline phosphatase’s unique clinical role
item.description: Background Cardiovascular-Kidney-Metabolic (CKM) syndrome involves disordered mineralization processes. Herein, we investigated the serum alkaline phosphatase (ALP), calcium, and phosphorus across CKM stages. Methods This study analyzed 15,233 eligible part…

item.title: Ingrid katz: what would it take to eradicate hiv?
item.description: Ingrid Katz: What Would It Take to Eradicate HIV?Anonymous (not verified) Thu, 06/18/2026 - 08:06 Howie and Harlan are joined by Ingrid Katz, director of the Yale Institute for Global Health, to discuss why HIV continues to spread despite the existence of c…

item.title: Openai is bringing on some big guns in the lead-up to its ipo | techcrunch
item.description: OpenAI is bulking up before its IPO, landing Transformer co-inventor Noam Shazeer from Google DeepMind and former Trump AI policy official Dean Ball in the same week.

item.title: The download: a new hunt for dark matter and kenya’s case for going solar
item.description: This is today’s edition of The Download, our weekday newsletter that provides a daily dose of what’s going on in the world of technology. The search for dark matter has been blown wide open For decades, physicists have hunted for weakly interacting massive pa…

item.title: The 11 standout startups from yc's demo day, according to vcs | techcrunch
item.description: TechCrunch spoke to investors to find the hottest startups in the Spring 2026 YC batch. Some of them commanded valuations of over $175 million, VCs said.

item.title: Amazon hopes to challenge nvidia more directly by selling its AI chips | techcrunch
item.description: AWS is in talks to sell its chips to other data centers. CEO Andy Jassy has said this represents a $50 billion opportunity for the company.

item.title: Adobe’s redesigned AI studio remembers what your creations look like
item.description: Adobe is introducing some new capabilities for its Firefly AI assistant, alongside a "reimagined" AI studio that lets you edit and generate new designs from a single interface. The new Firefly experience launching today in private beta is designed to give you…

item.title: EpiMII: Structure-Aware Graph Neural Networks for MHC-II Epitope Generation
item.description: EpiMII is a computational tool utilizing structure-aware graph neural networks to predict and generate major histocompatibility complex class II (MHC-II) epitopes. The approach integrates structural information through graph-based machine learning to improve epitope identification, supporting immuno

item.title: Advances in microneedle design for the delivery of drugs, proteins, and cells in the treatment of hypertrophic scars
item.description: This review examines advances in microneedle design for transdermal and intradermal delivery of drugs, proteins, and cells as therapeutic approaches for hypertrophic scar treatment. Microneedle technology offers minimally invasive delivery mechanisms that can enhance drug penetration and localized t

item.title: Fulcrumsec targets novo nordisk, leaks clinical and research data
item.description: FulcrumSec leaked data stolen from Novo Nordisk, claiming to have exfiltrated 1.3TB, including clinical records and AI research assets. On June 15, 2026, a data-theft extortion group calling itself FulcrumSec began leaking files from Novo Nordisk, the Danish …

item.title: Do we want to lead or lag in life sciences?
item.description: Life sciences leadership depends on strong ecosystems that connect science, capital and scale. Europe must do far more to support and incentivize innovation

item.title: Cygnvs launches command center for crises caused by a company’s own AI
item.description: Cyber resilience company CYGNVS Inc. today launched its AI Incident Command Center, a platform built to help organizations manage operational crises caused by their own artificial intelligence deployments. The product extends CYGNVS’ existing out-of-band inci…
